steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content:
The blank sits between the noun profile and the prepositional phrase in the Navajo Times. A word in this position modifies the noun directly, so it must be a participle (an adjective-like verb form), not a finite verb that would need its own subject.
  • Choice A — 'appearing': present participle — modifies profile as a reduced relative clause (profile that appeared) ✓
  • Choice B — 'appears': finite present-tense verb — cannot modify a noun directly ✗
  • Choice C — 'has appeared': finite present-perfect verb — cannot modify a noun directly ✗
  • Choice D — 'appeared': finite past-tense verb — cannot modify a noun directly ✗

steps.title: Confirm the correct choice
steps.content:
Only a participle can slot between a noun and a prepositional phrase to form a modifier. Appearing is the only participle among the options; the other three are all finite verbs that would create a grammatically broken sentence.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
